course_title: Special Topics in Philosophy: Spec Stud:19th/20th Cent Art
subject: PHIL
course_description: Selected topics and problems in philosophy. Content will vary in successive offerings of this course. May be repeated for credit when topic varies.
course_notes: (Same as ARTHIST 369R 1) By the late 20th century, architects and urbanists' unlikely exchanges with philosophers had become intense, prolific, radical, innovative, and widespread like never before. Their roles overlapped as they read one another, talked to each other, and at times even worked together. They produced iconic essays, projects, conferences, debates, and collaborations that fundamentally transformed architectural and urban discourse and practice alike. But what did designers and philosophers have to gain from crossing disciplinary borders? How have philosophical notions influenced architecture and urbanism? How have designers contributed to philosophical thought? In this seminar, we will dive into the theories and ideas that animated these exchanges. Understand their stories, actors, contexts, approaches, methodologies, proposals, and their effects then and today. Art History major/minor: Division III Architectural Studies minor: elective
